pi_items.source: verifier.py line 55 defines acceptable_states = ['approve', 'post', 'done']; Odoo 17 documentation shows these correspond to user-visible states: 'Approved', 'Posted', 'Done'
docker-compose.yml (referenced in setup_odoo.sh) uses image: odoo:17

pi_items.verified_value: Yes, Eli Lambert is employee #7 in Odoo's official 20-person HR demo dataset
pi_items.source: setup_data.py lines 6-10 document the official Odoo demo data; setup_task.sh line 35 confirms 'Employee Eli Lambert not found' would error if employee was missing

privileged_info_summary: The approve_expense_report task uses Odoo 17 Community Edition at http://localhost:8069 with admin/admin credentials and database odoo_hr. The task requires approving an expense report for Eli Lambert (a real demo employee from Odoo's official HR data) titled 'Q4 Digital Marketing Conference' containing three synthetic expense items: Conference Registration ($350.00), Hotel Accommodation ($480.00), and Meals ($95.00), totaling $925.00. The setup script creates the report in 'submitted' state; the agent must change it to 'approve' state (user-facing label: 'Approved'). The verifier confirms success by checking: (1) report exists in DB, (2) state is 'approve'/'post'/'done', (3) employee matches, (4) record ID matches original (no deletion/recreation), (5) total amount equals $925.00.
